title: Determine the exact value of R(K3, K4-e, K4-e)
code_used: bounded heuristic local search on 20 and 21 vertices plus an exact one-vertex extension checker for any recovered 20-vertex witness; no valid witness recovered in the bounded runs
micro_paper_assessment: Still unresolved and not paper-ready. A genuine exact solve would still be micro-paper shaped, but this run added only heuristic search evidence and no new exact theorem slice beyond the inherited pure-circulant obstruction.
family_affinity: Strong small-graph three-color Ramsey residue around R(K3, J4, J4), with direct survey-line leverage if the exact value closes.
generalization_signal: Neighborhood-matching and extension-partition arguments scale naturally in J4-family Ramsey problems, but naive local search appears too weak to recover extremal witnesses even at the known lower-bound size.
candidate_theorem_slice: Inherited exact slice only: pure circulant distance-colorings do not produce witnesses for 17 <= n <= 21; this run produced no stronger exact subtheorem.
next_action: Obtain one explicit valid 20-vertex witness and run the exact one-vertex extension checker on it, or isolate the sharp small two-color threshold needed to force 21 via the neighborhood-matching reduction.
